_draft_note: INERT DRAFT. Lives under godot/data/_drafts/ so DialogueRunner._load_all_dialogues (which uses DirAccess on godot/data/dialogues only, no recursion) never sees it. Date-suffix '2026-05-17' is extra defense in depth: even if hand-copied up one level, the filename ('ch1_murrow_2026-05-17.json') does not collide with the canonical 'murrow.json' dispatch key, so it would be loaded under npc_id='ch1_murrow_2026-05-17' and only fire for an NPC node addressing that id (none exists). State ids prefixed murrow_b{N}_ globally unique vs. current dialogues/*.json (rg verified 2026-05-17). All canonical Cula/Murrow address-form rules per godot/AGENTS.md §'Address forms in dialogue' and narrative_revision/voice_agents/murrow.md §'Address forms (strict)'. The Beat 4 first-name invitation pair (Murrow's 'It is Murrow, to friends...' + Asia-hedge 'I am still deciding...') is verbatim canonical staging.
_chapter_ref: narrative_revision/beats/chapter_1.md — Beats 4, 8, 9, 10, 13. Article 135-bis § 2 KPC clause text quoted verbatim from Beat 9 spec.

states._comment: Beat 4 opener. Source spec: narrative_revision/beats/chapter_1.md §Beat 4 ('eviction briefing + Procedural Binder + first-name beat'). Voice anchor: murrow.md §'How Murrow talks' (cite + ironic gloss + directive; protective course-correction; institutional history aside). Murrow names Halina, the renumbering, the wrong-address mechanism, and the 14-day actual-notice clock — all per Beat-4 substantive-content list. Address forms (godot/AGENTS.md §'Address forms in dialogue'): pre-invitation, Murrow opens with 'Dr. A. Cula' (not 'Doctor Cula' — the cite uses canonical reference form in formal first words); Cula must still say 'Mr. Murrow' in this state because the invitation has not yet landed (it lands in murrow_b4_first_name). Eight NPC lines exactly (Rule A ceiling). Two jokes (filing-system irony in line 5; the 'fiction the envelope reached the human' fiction-naming in line 6). Single archival adjective per line — load-bearing 'misdirected' (line 4) is the only adjective in the run. No contrastive antithesis. No em-dashes in player-facing text.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Welcome to Pig & Swine. You have picked a noisy day to begin.
The eviction case. Client is Halina Sikorska. Pre-war building, central Warsaw, tenancy inherited from her father in 1987.
In 2015 the landlord renumbered the block. Her flat went from seven to five. The eviction notice was served to the current resident of the old number seven.
She got the misdirected notice three weeks late. By then the hearing had already happened, with no one in the room to defend her.

Article 135-bis paragraph two KPC is the article you want. The post office's least favorite, the court's depending on the judge. The relevant copy sits in the procedural binder on the second shelf, behind URGENT 2011, which is itself a piece of evidence about how Mr. Pig understands the word urgent.

Service of process is the law's polite fiction that the envelope reached the human. The clock for the motion to set aside runs fourteen days from actual notice, not original service. We are inside the window.
She has not formally retained yet. She is coming in this afternoon for the meeting, which is when retention happens and the fee is named.
Pick up the binder before you speak with Crab. He will read it with you and find what he finds; the shape of the argument is yours to draw.

choices.text: (Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. Have you seen this kind of case before?)
(Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. How long have you been at Pig & Swine?)
(Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. What should I expect from the client?)

states._comment: Beat 4 character-dwell A. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 4 'Have you seen this kind of case before?' dwell — terse-instructional register. Voice anchor: 'More than I would like. The renumbering is unusual; the wrong-address tactic is not.' Address: still pre-invitation, Murrow uses 'Dr. A. Cula' (formal form, in lieu of the bare 'Doctor Cula' used elsewhere — both are within the bible's allowed range pre-invitation, and the cite-form reads more like Murrow's archival mode). Chain back to murrow_b4_first_name which carries the invitation and the on_dismiss met_murrow flip. Rule B compliance: option leads within one beat to the first-name landing, which is the player-visible event.
lines: More than I would like. The renumbering is unusual; the wrong-address tactic is not.
Landlords in pre-war stock have been losing tenancy disputes on service for about a decade. They have learned to be careless on purpose. The bench is starting to notice.

choices.text: (Mr. Murrow. Thank you. Where is the binder again?)

states._comment: Beat 4 character-dwell B. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 4 'How long have you worked here?' dwell — archival-aside register. Surfaces archival depth without surfacing chapter-4 ledger content (per phase-7 voice-references spec line 432, the 'archival depth' bound is real). Address: pre-invitation 'Dr. A. Cula'. Two jokes: 'before the firm had a printer' (specific archival fact), 'the same way Mr. Pig has' (collegial-dry; protective frame about Pig). Single archival adjective per line: 'reproducible' (line 2) is the only adjective in the run.
lines: Since the firm's founding period, Dr. A. Cula. Before the firm had a printer, which is a fact that becomes funnier and worse with each year I spend here.

Mr. Pig and Mr. Swine hired me to file things. The hiring decision turned out to be reproducible across several subsequent juniors. You are the most recent application of the pattern, the same way Mr. Pig has been the most consistent.

choices.text: (Mr. Murrow. The binder, then. Second shelf.)

states._comment: Beat 4 character-dwell C. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 4 'What should I expect from the client?' dwell — dry-collegial register. Primes Cula's stance choices for Beat 8 (per spec). Address: pre-invitation 'Dr. A. Cula'. One joke: the 'talked down to in three languages' line lands as wry knowledge of teachers. Single archival adjective: 'considered' (line 1) is load-bearing.
lines: Mrs. Sikorska is a retired teacher. Polish-language teacher, secondary school, thirty-eight years on the same staff list. She will be considered. She will have done her own reading. She will not appreciate being talked down to in three languages, of which two will be hers.
Take her documents seriously as documents. She brought a folder; the folder is the message before she opens her mouth. The rest is for you to find.

states._comment: Beat 4 first-name landing. The canonical pair (Piotr-locked 2026-05-17): Murrow's friend-invitation + Asia-hedge, verbatim from chapter_1.md §Phase-8 decisions #7 and murrow.md §'Address forms (strict)'. Cula's acceptance line 'Then it's Cula.' is the player-visible commit on the address-form shift. After this state, Cula is licensed to drop 'Mr.' (the NPC node's display_name_after_meeting export drives the swap; the friend-invitation flag is met_murrow itself per existing convention in murrow.json). Murrow's directive at the end keeps the briefing on the rails. Eight NPC lines exactly (Rule A ceiling) including the Asia-hedge interject. Two jokes: 'Mister I keep for invoices' (institution-as-billable joke) and 'workplace constellations' (the comic noun substitution that makes the hedge land). on_dismiss sets met_murrow plus the three required elements established during the briefing (E3, E4 fully surfaced; the timeline/window/no-authority bools also flip — these are the Beat-4 fact-flags that gate Beat-9's clause-by-clause and the post-packet motion-vehicle line in the existing murrow.json, all canonical flags from the brief's whitelist).
lines: Behind URGENT 2011, second shelf, blue spine. The rights memo is clipped inside the front cover; do not lose it between here and Crab's desk.
It is Murrow, to friends. The 'Mister' I keep for invoices.
I am still deciding whether reception staff qualify as friends or as workplace constellations.

lines.speaker: cula
lines._inline_comment: Cula's reply tilts dry; he does not fight the joke.

lines: Cula. Binder first. Crab second. Halina at one. If anything in the binder reads wrong, find me before then.
And do not bring the rights memo to Mr. Pig before lunch. He reads worse on an empty stomach.

states._comment: Beat 8 Pig-shepherding moment. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 8 'Pig's brief presence (open question, recommended yes). Pig enters in panic-register, says two lines that are inappropriate to the room (maritime-flavored about the firm's survival), then is shepherded out. The brief presence plants Pig's chronic mode for chapter-5 retraversal — even the client meeting cannot suspend his panic.' Murrow's shepherding line: dry-collegial; protective course-correction shape (the move is the ushering, the words frame it as ordinary). Address: post-Beat-4, Murrow is using 'Cula' in private — but the meeting room is a client-facing context, so he stays formal ('Dr. A. Cula') in front of Mrs. Sikorska, per the voice bible: 'Cula in private and Dr. A. Cula in formal contexts (court, in front of clients).' Pig speaker interject is allowed by registry (pig:Mr. Pig). Five NPC lines including the in-state Murrow/Pig swap — well under Rule A's 8-line ceiling. Two jokes: 'inheriting his maritime register' (institutional aside) and the polite-fiction reframe of Pig's exit ('we will rejoin you in a moment') as if it were Pig's plan all along.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. The rent is taking on water. We are six weeks under the surface. But the firm is, structurally, still present.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: And the printer lease has come back as a kraken. We renew next month, presumably with bait.

lines: Mr. Pig. Asia has the call sheet on your desk; we will rejoin you in a moment.
Mrs. Sikorska, my apologies. The firm has weather, and Mr. Pig has been inheriting his maritime register from it for nine years. Dr. A. Cula, the chair to your right.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska. Dr. A. Cula. Please sit; we'll take this in order.

states._comment: Beat 8 intervention. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 8 'Murrow's intervention lines. Dry-collegial. If a stance line goes somewhere unproductive, Murrow redirects with a single line. Sample shape: The notice. (Returning the conversation to the document.)' This state is the catch-net; it fires when the player has met Halina but the fee/retention sequence has not yet locked, which is the unproductive-drift window where redirection makes sense. Two short Murrow interjections — single archival adjective load-bearing per line ('current' and 'inheritance'). Two jokes: 'the document is more reliable than any of us in this room' (file-as-witness joke, classic Murrow framing) and 'including, on a good day, the document itself' (the irony-on-itself shape that names the law's polite fiction without solving it). Address: in front of client, Murrow stays formal. Three NPC lines, including a Cula interject. Rule B: option commits write to state_choice with chain-back behavior signaling fee-agreement gate has been navigated; the next state (b8_fee_close) lands the canonical 5,000 PLN.
lines: Mrs. Sikorska, the notice. May we have it on the table.
The document is more reliable than any of us in this room, including, on a good day, the document itself.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mrs. Sikorska, the envelope. When did you actually have it in your hand?

choices.text: (Lead with sympathetic register: how is she holding up.)
(Lead with technical register: the inheritance paperwork.)

states._comment: Beat 8 fee close. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 8 'Fee discussion. Murrow or Cula raises the fee. Halina has calculated; she names what she can afford; the figure is 5,000 PLN; the firm accepts. Not a player choice.' Murrow handles the fee-naming move because (a) he is the only fully-qualified lawyer in the room and (b) the firm-survival framing for chapter 13 lands more cleanly with a Murrow-anchored on-record moment. Stance affects the on_dismiss client_meeting_stance write; the cardiologist-plant flag flips here regardless (the plant is stance-invariant per spec). One-time client_meeting_evidence and halina_arrived/halina_met flags presumed set elsewhere; this state's job is fee + stance + cardiologist. Address: 'Mrs. Sikorska' (full honorific, per bible); 'Dr. A. Cula' (client-facing). Two jokes: the 'fixed fee — both words load-bearing' irony and 'we accept' as the single-syllable transaction-marker that lands the fee against the bigger thematic weight. Single archival adjective per line: 'fixed' (line 2) is the joke-load-bearing one. Six lines including two Halina interjects from a speaker registered as 'halina'.
lines: Then on the matter of fee. Mrs. Sikorska, the firm's standard arrangement.

lines.speaker: halina
lines.text: I have calculated. Five thousand zloty, fixed. Paid before the hearing.

lines: Fixed fee. Both words load-bearing in this firm. We accept.

lines.speaker: halina
lines.text: I have also had to reschedule a cardiology follow-up. It is nothing; I have been managing this for years.

lines: Noted. Mrs. Sikorska, on Friday we will collect you at the building entrance at thirteen-thirty. Dr. A. Cula will lead the motion; I will sit second chair.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you, Mrs. Sikorska. The team will see this through.

states._comment: Beat 9 archive research opener. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 9. Murrow opens the Procedural Binder, frames the article, hands Cula the routing for the three clauses. The full Art. 135-bis § 2 KPC text is quoted in line 2 (verbatim from beat sheet); Murrow's narration begins in line 3 with the first-clause apply-the-rule move. Address: post-Beat-4, archive room is private (only Cula present), so Murrow uses 'Cula'. The on_dismiss chain into clause_1 happens via a chain:true option; the player-visible commit is 'Read the first clause' which lands the invalid-service definition. Splitting into three states (clause_1 / clause_2 / clause_3) per Rule A — each clause runs three to four Murrow sentences plus Cula interject, well under the 8-line ceiling. Two jokes: 'fashionable again because the post office decided to stop pretending' (institutional-history aside, vintage Murrow) and 'green pen because the green pen was the only one that worked that month' (filing-system irony). Single archival adjective: 'green' (line 4) is the only one in the run.
lines: Cula. The article. Read it once aloud; you will misremember it less.

Article 135-bis paragraph two. Service is invalid when the address used has been altered, renumbered, or otherwise made non-current to the knowledge of the serving party. A hearing held upon invalid service shall, on motion of the party not properly served, be set aside and rescheduled, provided the motion is filed within fourteen days of actual notice. The appearance of a third party at the served address shall not be considered as curing service unless that party held the express authority of the served party.
Drafted in 2003. Ignored for about eight years. Fashionable again because the post office decided to stop pretending things had been delivered.

Three clauses. The first is invalid service; the second is the motion plus the fourteen-day window; the third pre-empts the cure-by-third-party defense. I have the doctrinal notes in green pen because the green pen was the only one that worked that month.

choices.text: (Read the first clause with him: invalid service.)

states._comment: Beat 9 first clause: invalid-service definition. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 9 'First clause (invalid-service definition). Murrow walks through altered, renumbered, or otherwise made non-current. Names the 2015 renumbering. Names the landlord's knowledge (the landlord authorized the renumbering — the knowledge requirement is met).' Five Murrow lines + one Cula interject (Rule A: under 8 NPC). on_dismiss flips element_landlord_knowledge AND element_non_current_address — these are the two factual hooks the first clause establishes. Voice: cite + ironic gloss + directive (the directive lands in line 5: hold the knowledge requirement). Two jokes: 'three terms doing different jobs' (the law-as-categorization frame) and 'the landlord signed his own knowledge requirement' (the on-record irony, dry). Single archival adjective: 'non-current' (line 2) reads as procedural-technical with an angle — the only adjective in the run with rhetorical load.
lines: Clause one. Altered, renumbered, or otherwise made non-current to the knowledge of the serving party. Three terms doing different jobs.

Altered covers the obvious. The address changed and the server used the old version. Renumbered is the 2015 mass-renumbering of pre-war stock; her flat went from seven to five. Otherwise made non-current is the catch-all the courts use when a landlord cannot decide which kind of bad faith he is performing.
Knowledge of the serving party. The 2019 renewal in the file carries the landlord's countersignature at the bottom of page two. He signed the document that records the new number.
He cannot now plead surprise about a renumbering he authorized. The landlord signed his own knowledge requirement.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: So the first clause closes on knowledge. We hold that against the renewal page two.

lines: Yes. Hold the knowledge requirement against the renewal signature. That is the first element on the page.

choices.text: (On to clause two: the fourteen-day window.)

states._comment: Beat 9 second clause: motion + 14-day actual-notice window. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 9 'Second clause (motion + 14-day window). Murrow walks through the actual-notice clock. Counts: Halina received the misdirected notice on [specific date]; today is [date]; Pig & Swine has [N] days remaining. The clock is tight but the firm is inside the window.' I lock the dates to canonical fixture: actual notice 28 April (per the existing murrow.json murrow_first_meeting line), Friday hearing per the binder note. Math: from 28 April + 14 days = 12 May; the chapter-1 case files the motion before Friday's hearing. Four Murrow lines + Cula interject; under Rule A. Voice: the explanation pattern — cite, ironic gloss, who-cares-about-this institutional note, directive. Two jokes: 'a fortnight or a procedural eternity, depending on which side of it you are standing on' (time-as-law irony) and 'the registry needs the morning to lose the form so that you can produce a copy in the afternoon' (institutional-history aside, vintage Murrow). Single archival adjective: 'tight' (line 4) is load-bearing for the urgency.
lines: Clause two. Motion to set aside, on the motion of the party not properly served, filed within fourteen days of actual notice.

Actual notice, not original service. The clock starts when the document actually reaches the human, which is a phrase the courts have spent fifty years arguing about and still write into statutes with a straight face.

Mrs. Sikorska received the misdirected notice on the 28th of April. Fourteen days runs to the 12th of May. Today is the 6th. Friday's hearing is the 9th; we file the motion no later than Thursday afternoon so the registry has time to lose it and produce a copy in the morning.
Inside the window. Tight, in the way fourteen days are always a fortnight or a procedural eternity, depending on which side of it you are standing on.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thursday by close of business. The packet carries the dates on its face.

states._comment: Beat 9 third clause: third-party non-cure. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 9 'Third clause (third-party non-cure). Murrow walks through the preempted defense. The current renter of #7 is not Halina's authorized agent. The landlord's likely but they accepted the notice argument fails on its face.' This is the on-record E4 surfacing (already set in murrow_first_meeting; the on_dismiss here flips archive_research_complete, closing Beat 9). Four Murrow lines + Cula commit; under Rule A. Voice: naming-the-fiction move ('the law agrees that handing the envelope to a neighbor counts as handing it to the human') — vintage Murrow, lifted from the voice bible. Two jokes: the polite-fiction frame in line 2 and the dry 'the binder is heavy enough that it has its own weather system' in line 4 — that adjective phrase is doing double work as Murrow institutional-tic AND inventory callback (matches the Procedural Binder's item description per Beat 4 spec). Single archival adjective: 'express' (line 3) is load-bearing for the cure question.
lines: Clause three. Appearance of a third party at the served address does not cure service unless that party held the express authority of the served party.

Substitute service is the doctrine that handing the envelope to a neighbor counts as handing it to the human. The neighbor, of course, was not consulted. The clause preempts that fiction in this kind of case.
The current resident of number seven brought her the notice three weeks late. He held no authority from Mrs. Sikorska, express or otherwise. He was a stranger with a piece of paper and a stairwell.

We have the four elements. Address non-current, landlord on notice of the change, motion within the window, no third party with express authority. The binder is heavy enough that it has its own weather system; the case theory is now lighter than the binder.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Then it goes Friday. I will draft the packet tonight.

states._comment: Beat 10 slimmed readiness check. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 10 'Phase-8 slims this beat because case content is now established in Beats 8-9. The readiness check focuses on mechanics: court schedule, who carries what evidence, motion timing.' This state replaces the existing murrow.json court_readiness_check for the consolidated-draft layout (the existing state covers similar ground; the b10 version is leaner and shifts the Pig printer-lease and Pig 'back in my day' moments into this slot per spec phase-6 distribution). Five Murrow lines (Rule A) plus interjects from Pig and Asia. The opener uses 'Cula' (post-Beat-4, private among the team; Halina is not in the room yet). Two jokes: Pig's 'navigated by panic alone' lecture (canonical, per beat sheet sample shape; sincere on first pass) and Murrow's 'the form will lose itself' procedural inevitability. Single archival adjective per Murrow line: 'mechanical' (line 1), 'second' (line 3), 'thirteen-thirty' (line 4 — numeric, not adjectival, so doesn't count), 'standing' (line 5). Each is load-bearing. Address rule: Murrow says 'Cula'; Asia keeps 'Mr. Murrow' (canonical hedge); Cula's interject uses 'Murrow' (post-Beat-4 invitation accepted).
lines: Cula. The case content is established. This is the mechanical pass.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: The printer lease has come back as a kraken. We renew next month, presumably with bait.

lines: Procedural binder on file. Rights memo on file. Motion drafted and lodged with the registry; the form will lose itself and the copy will be in your hand by Friday morning.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: When I was an apprentice, we had no readiness check. Only fear. We navigated by panic alone.

lines: Crab carries the service evidence. Whimsy carries the rights memo and is under instructions to keep the metaphors short of the bench. We leave the office at thirteen-thirty, courtroom four, hearing at fourteen hundred.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Murrow. The motion order at the bench?

lines: Service first, fair hearing second, remedy last. You will lead. I will sit second chair, standing only if the bench asks for the article cite on its own. Mrs. Sikorska meets us at the building entrance at thirteen forty-five.

lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: She rang an hour ago, Mr. Murrow. She'll be there at quarter to.

states._comment: Beat 13 post-ledger brief line. Spec: chapter_1.md §Beat 13 'Brief Murrow post-update line to Cula (phase-8 addition). Dry-collegial register. One sentence acknowledging the win without celebrating it. Recommended shape: a checklist-style remark about the next case (sets up the chapter close).' The ledger-update visual itself (drawer open, ledger out, notation, ledger back, no dialogue) is silent staging and does not live in this dialogue tree — it is scene-level. This state is the brief verbal line AFTER the silent ledger moment. Two Murrow lines: the win-acknowledgment with-no-celebration, and the next-case directive that closes the chapter on Murrow's voice. Address: 'Cula' (post-Beat-4, private, end-of-day in the office). One joke: 'against several predictions, including some of mine' — vintage Murrow self-irony from existing voice-reference (ch01_036 in the JSONL). Single archival adjective: 'coherent' (line 1) is load-bearing — Murrow's measured calibration of the win as procedurally clean rather than triumphant. No on_dismiss flag write (the chapter-close flag is engine-side, set by other systems).
lines: Cula. Against several predictions, including some of mine, we now have a coherent result. Mrs. Sikorska sleeps in her own flat tonight; the firm survives the month.
There is a name on the docket for Tuesday. Read the intake form before you leave. Whoever drafted it has done so under the impression that adjectives are evidence.

idle_flavor: The paperwork is current. Whether it is correct is a separate question.
Two pages are out of sequence. Not yours.
There is a Mrozek line for this. I will not quote it; the situation is doing it for me.
